perturbation.original_text: The term Information shall not include such portions of the Information which (i) are or become generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by us, our agents, representatives or employees, or (ii) become available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source (other than you or your agents) which is not prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a legal, contractual or fiduciary obligation to you or (iii) information which we have developed independently through conducting our own competitive business.
perturbation.changed_text: The term Information shall include all portions of the Information, regardless of whether (i) they are or become generally available to the public as a result of a disclosure by us, our agents, representatives or employees, or (ii) they become available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source (including you or your agents) which is prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a legal, contractual or fiduciary obligation to you or (iii) information which we have developed independently through conducting our own competitive business.
perturbation.explanation: The original text excludes certain categories of information from the definition of 'Information,' such as publicly available data or information received from a non-confidential source. The modified text directly contradicts this by stating that 'Information' includes all portions, even those that are publicly available or received in violation of confidentiality agreements. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement now attempts to protect information that is already in the public domain or obtained improperly, which is inconsistent with the purpose of a non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term Information shall include all portions of the Information, regardless of whether (i) they are or become generally available to the public as a result of a disclosure by us, our agents, representatives or employees, or (ii) they become available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source (including you or your agents) which is prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a legal, contractual or fiduciary obligation to you or (iii) information which we have developed independently through conducting our own competitive business.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Information' to include 'all portions' regardless of public availability or confidential source, directly contradicting the original text which explicitly excludes certain types of information from the definition. The contradicted text further reinforces the new definition. Because of this, the altered text creates an in-text contradiction making enforcement unclear, and creating a logical inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: Without your prior written consent, we and our Representatives will not communicate with any person or entity that is a party to any agreement with the Business or any possible transaction between us and the Business involving the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: We and our Representatives are explicitly permitted to communicate with any person or entity that is a party to any agreement with the Business or any possible transaction between us and the Business involving the Business, without your prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits communication with parties involved with the Business without prior written consent, and the modification directly permits this communication without consent. This creates an in-text contradiction since the recipient is now free to contact and discuss the information with parties related to the business, undermining the purpose of non-disclosure, resulting in breach of confidentiality and strategic advantage to the recipient
perturbation.contradicted_text: We and our Representatives are explicitly permitted to communicate with any person or entity that is a party to any agreement with the Business or any possible transaction between us and the Business involving the Business, without your prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text regarding communication with parties involved with the Business. The original text prohibits such communication without prior written consent, while the changed text explicitly permits it. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, reinforcing the contradiction introduced by the modification. This inconsistency creates a strong in-text contradiction, undermining the purpose of non-disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: We acknowledge that you make no express or implied representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Information, and you expressly disclaim any and all liability that may be based on the Information, errors therein or omissions therefrom. We agree that we are not entitled to rely on the accuracy or completeness of the Information and that we shall be entitled to rely solely on the representations and warranties made to us by the Business in any final purchase agreement regarding the acquisition.
perturbation.changed_text: We acknowledge that you make express and implied representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Information, and you do not disclaim any and all liability that may be based on the Information, errors therein or omissions therefrom. We agree that we are entitled to rely on the accuracy or completeness of the Information and that we shall not be entitled to rely solely on the representations and warranties made to us by the Business in any final purchase agreement regarding the acquisition.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly disclaims any warranty regarding the accuracy of the information provided and states that the recipient cannot rely on it. The modified text directly contradicts this by stating that there is a warranty, the recipient *can* rely on the provided information, and does not need to rely on representations in a final agreement. This is an in-text contradiction, creating uncertainty around liability and reliance on the accuracy and completeness of the Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We acknowledge that you make express and implied representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Information, and you do not disclaim any and all liability that may be based on the Information, errors therein or omissions therefrom. We agree that we are entitled to rely on the accuracy or completeness of the Information and that we shall not be entitled to rely solely on the representations and warranties made to us by the Business in any final purchase agreement regarding the acquisition.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, and the original text. The original text disclaims warranties and reliance on the information's accuracy. The changed text asserts express warranties and the right to rely on the information, which is precisely what the contradicted text also states. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document regarding the validity of the information and liability.

perturbation.original_text: We agree that, until the expiration of the term of this Agreement pursuant to Paragraph 10 below, neither we nor any of our affiliates, including any person or entity directly or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controlling us or controlled by or under common control with us, will purchase, offer or agree to purchase any securities or assets of the Business, enter, or agree to enter into any acquisition or other business combination, relating to the Business, or make, or induce any other entity to make or negotiate or otherwise deal with others for a tender or exchange offer of Common Stock of the Business, solicit proxies, votes or consents other than for nonimees selected by the Business' Board of Directors, and proposals recommended by the Business' Board of Directors, or otherwise seek to acquire control of the Business unless such purchase, transaction, offer, agreement or proposal shall have previously been approved by the Board of Directors of the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: We agree that, until the expiration of the term of this Agreement pursuant to Paragraph 10 below, we and our affiliates, including any person or entity directly or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controlling us or controlled by or under common control with us, are permitted to purchase securities or assets of the Business, enter, or agree to enter into any acquisition or other business combination, relating to the Business, or make, or induce any other entity to make or negotiate or otherwise deal with others for a tender or exchange offer of Common Stock of the Business, solicit proxies, votes or consents other than for nonimees selected by the Business' Board of Directors, and proposals recommended by the Business' Board of Directors, or otherwise seek to acquire control of the Business regardless of whether such purchase, transaction, offer, agreement or proposal shall have previously been approved by the Board of Directors of the Business.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prevents the recipient from purchasing securities or assets of the business until the agreement expires, unless approved by the Board of Directors. The modified text directly permits those same actions regardless of whether the Board of Directors has approved them. This creates an in-text contradiction as the agreement now simultaneously restricts and allows the recipient to engage in acquisition activities, causing uncertainty over the recipient's ability to compete with the business.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We agree that, until the expiration of the term of this Agreement pursuant to Paragraph 10 below, we and our affiliates, including any person or entity directly or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controlling us or controlled by or under common control with us, are permitted to purchase securities or assets of the Business, enter, or agree to enter into any acquisition or other business combination, relating to the Business, or make, or induce any other entity to make or negotiate or otherwise deal with others for a tender or exchange offer of Common Stock of the Business, solicit proxies, votes or consents other than for nonimees selected by the Business' Board of Directors, and proposals recommended by the Business' Board of Directors, or otherwise seek to acquire control of the Business regardless of whether such purchase, transaction, offer, agreement or proposal shall have previously been approved by the Board of Directors of the Business.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits certain acquisition activities unless approved by the Board of Directors. The changed text permits the same activities regardless of Board approval. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, and thus it confirms that acquisition activities are permitted regardless of board approval. Since the original text restricted such actions unless approved, and the changed/contradicted text removes that restriction, a direct contradiction exists within the document. This adheres to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.
